Aniline Fukui-reactivity (most electrophilic site) — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitting) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: single-point calculation on the user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); no geometry optimization was performed. Partial charges and Fukui functions were obtained via Mulliken analysis from single-point energies of the neutral (N), cationic (N–1), and anionic (N+1) states at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level. The most electrophilic atom is C atom 3 (1-based labeling) with f⁺ = 2.2975867. SCF converged for all states. Warnings (verbatim):
- sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
- sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
